I don't know what kind of apples they are but the church here has planted a bunch of apple trees. I'm sure if you wanted to you could go to the church and ask him if you could take some and it wouldn't be a problem.
Tall, mature trees near a creak opening next to the hike path. Hundreds of dropped nuts every fall. Look for huge green lemony smelling pods on the ground Access: Public.
Scattered along the walkway. Many are in high branches but there’s also a substantial amount within reach. A bit tart but I’ve used them for wine that came out great. Access: Public.
Large mature shagbark hickory trees. Can gather nuts in the fall Access: Public.
Large mature shagbark hickory trees. Can gather nuts in the fall Access: Public.

I don't know what kind of pear tree this is but it's in the backyard of the 8th house on Bellemeadow Drive. It hangs over the backyard into the Alpine Elementary fields. Look for all of the Rose of Sharon bushes and it should be above that. Access: Private but
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
